disclaimer: Reference information only — not medical advice, diagnosis, or prescription. Seek professional care when possible.

items.name: Antiseptic wipes
items.what_it_is_for: Cleaning skin around a wound before dressing; reducing contamination risk.
items.how_to_use_safely: Wipe outward from the wound edge once; do not reuse wipes; avoid inside deep wounds or eyes.
items.warning: Reference info only. If the wound is deep, spurting, or heavily contaminated, prioritize bleeding control and evacuation.

items.name: Sterile gauze / dressing
items.what_it_is_for: Covering wounds and absorbing blood after bleeding is controlled.
items.how_to_use_safely: Apply direct pressure through gauze; replace when soaked; secure without cutting off circulation.
items.warning: Does not replace pressure or tourniquet for life-threatening bleeding.

items.name: Commercial tourniquet
items.what_it_is_for: Stopping life-threatening limb bleeding when direct pressure fails.
items.how_to_use_safely: Place 2–3 inches above the wound toward the torso; tighten until bleeding stops; note time applied on the device or skin.
items.warning: Causes pain — that is expected. Do not remove once applied unless you are trained to do so and evacuation is imminent.

items.name: Adhesive bandages
items.what_it_is_for: Small cuts and abrasions after cleaning.
items.how_to_use_safely: Clean and dry the area; change if wet or dirty; watch for increased redness or swelling.
items.warning: Not for large, deep, or heavily bleeding wounds.

items.name: Oral pain reliever (general OTC)
items.what_it_is_for: May help with mild to moderate pain when appropriate for the person.
items.how_to_use_safely: Use only if the person can swallow safely and is not allergic. Follow the exact directions on the package label.
items.warning: Follow package directions; this is reference info, not a prescription; seek a medic when possible. Never guess a dose for someone else's condition.

items.name: Oral antihistamine (general OTC)
items.what_it_is_for: May help with mild allergic reactions such as itching or hives when breathing is normal.
items.how_to_use_safely: Use only if the person can swallow safely. Follow the exact directions on the package label.
items.warning: Follow package directions; reference info, not a prescription; seek a medic when possible. If breathing difficulty, swelling of face/throat, or wheezing — treat as emergency.

items.name: Oral rehydration salts (ORS)
items.what_it_is_for: Replacing fluids and electrolytes after dehydration from vomiting, diarrhea, or heat.
items.how_to_use_safely: Mix exactly as the packet label states; give small sips if the person is conscious and can swallow.
items.warning: Reference info only. If the person is confused, not urinating, or cannot keep fluids down — seek evacuation.

items.name: Burn dressing / gel
items.what_it_is_for: Covering minor burns after brief cooling with clean water.
items.how_to_use_safely: Cool small burns with clean water 10–20 minutes first; apply a sterile non-stick dressing loosely; do not use ice.
items.warning: Do not use on large or deep burns — cover loosely and treat for shock instead.

items.name: Medical tape
items.what_it_is_for: Securing dressings without relying on adhesive directly on the wound.
items.how_to_use_safely: Tape around the dressing edges; check fingers/toes for color and warmth after securing.
items.warning: Remove or loosen if numbness, tingling, or swelling occurs distal to the tape.

items.name: Antibiotic ointment (topical)
items.what_it_is_for: May reduce infection risk on minor clean cuts after rinsing.
items.how_to_use_safely: Apply a thin layer to the skin around a minor wound; cover with a clean dressing. Use only on intact skin margins, not inside deep punctures.
items.warning: Topical reference only — not for internal use. Follow package directions; seek a medic for deep, dirty, or animal bites.

items.name: Nitrile gloves
items.what_it_is_for: Barrier protection for you and the casualty during wound care.
items.how_to_use_safely: Don before contact with blood or body fluids; remove without touching the outer surface; wash hands after.
items.warning: Gloves reduce contamination risk but do not replace proper bleeding control technique.

items.name: Trauma shears / scissors
items.what_it_is_for: Cutting clothing away from injuries; trimming tape or dressings.
items.how_to_use_safely: Cut away from the body; keep tips blunt-side toward skin; expose the wound without pulling embedded objects.
items.warning: Do not probe wounds or remove impaled objects — expose and stabilize only.

items.name: Elastic bandage (ACE wrap)
items.what_it_is_for: Light support and holding dressings on limbs after bleeding is controlled.
items.how_to_use_safely: Wrap from distal to proximal with even pressure; leave toes/fingers visible to check circulation.
items.warning: Never use as a tourniquet substitute. Loosen if the limb becomes cold, blue, or numb.
